minutes_briefs.title: New mining reserves created on Cowichan River Park, Mt. Washington, and Tā Ch’Ilā Park land
minutes_briefs.summary: The Chief Gold Commissioner has added three areas to the list of mineral land reserves in British Columbia. This means no new mineral claims (permission to explore or mine for minerals) or placer claims (permission to mine for gold or other minerals in stream beds) can be registered on these sites. The affected areas are: Cowichan River Park Addition – site 3, Mt. Washington Gravel Pit Addition 1, and Tā Ch’Ilā Park (also known as Boya Lake Park).
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 82/2026
Made under: Mineral Tenure Act
Published in: No. 9 – B.C. Regs. 77 to 82, May 19, 2026
Deposit date: May 13, 2026

B.C. Reg. 82/2026, deposited May 13, 2026, under theMINERAL TENURE ACT[section 22]. Regulation of the Chief Gold Commissioner, dated May 13, 2026. I, Donna Myketa, Chief Gold Commissioner, Ministry of Mining and Critical Minerals, order as follows: (a)	the Mineral Land Reserve (No Mineral Claim Registrations) Regulation, B.C. Reg. 71/2010, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 1; (b)	the Mineral Land Reserve (No Mineral or Placer Claim Registrations) Regulation, B.C. Reg. 271/2007, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 2. — D. MYKETA,Chief Gold Commissioner, Ministry of Mining and Critical Minerals. 1	The Mineral Land Reserve (No Mineral Claim Registrations) Regulation, B.C. Reg. 71/2010, is amended by adding the following sites to Table 2: Mining Division and Land District New Westminster Mining Division Cowichan River Park Addition – site 3 Mt. Washington Gravel Pit Addition 1	The Mineral Land Reserve (No Mineral or Placer Claim Registrations) Regulation, B.C. Reg. 271/2007, is amended by adding the following site to Table 2: Mining Division and Land District Tā Ch’Ilā Park (a.k.a. Boya Lake Park) Index Page|Previous Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada
